title: Around the World in Eighty Days
description: Around the World in Eighty Days — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Around the World in Eighty Days" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Allahabad
memes.description: An Indian city at the confluence of the Ganges and Yamuna rivers, situated at the junction of the Great Indian Peninsula Railway line — the point where the interrupted railway from Bombay to Calcutta resumed after the fifty-mile gap from Kholby.

memes.label: America in the Novel
memes.description: Locations, transport routes, and events from the American portion of Phileas Fogg's journey, from San Francisco to New York and across the Atlantic.

memes.label: Andrew Stuart
memes.description: The Reform Club member and engineer who challenges Fogg on the feasibility of an eighty-day circumnavigation — unwittingly triggering the entire adventure — and who waits at the club until the final second for Fogg's return.

memes.label: Aouda
memes.description: The young Parsi woman rescued by Fogg and Passepartout from suttee in India — who travels with them to England, transforms Fogg's emotional life, and becomes his wife.

memes.label: The Route
memes.description: The precise eighty-day itinerary that Fogg follows around the globe — a route made possible by the Suez Canal, Indian railways, and American transcontinental railroad, each a recent triumph of nineteenth-century engineering.

memes.label: The Bank of England
memes.description: The institution whose robbery of fifty-five thousand pounds triggers Detective Fix's pursuit of the suspected thief -- whom he mistakenly identifies as Phileas Fogg.

memes.label: William Batulcar
memes.description: The Honourable William Batulcar, manager of a Japanese acrobatic troupe in Yokohama who employs Passepartout as a performer.

memes.label: Bombay
memes.description: The great Indian port city where Fogg disembarks from the Mongolia and boards the train for Calcutta — the gateway to the subcontinent and the first non-European station of the journey.

memes.label: Bradshaw's Guide
memes.description: The indispensable railway and steamship timetable that Phileas Fogg carried throughout his eighty-day journey — the printed embodiment of the global transportation network that made circumnavigation calculable, yet one whose limits were exposed by every gap, delay, and missed connection on the route.
aliases: Bradshaw's Continental Railway Steam Transit and General Guide

memes.label: Brigham Young
memes.description: Historical leader of the Mormon church, referenced during the train journey through Utah as the chief of the Latter-day Saints settlement at Salt Lake City.

memes.label: Brindisi
memes.description: The Italian port where Fogg boards the Mongolia for the Suez Canal -- the last European stop before the Orient.

memes.label: British Consular Service
memes.description: The network of British diplomatic offices whose passport visa requirements provided Detective Fix with the legal mechanism to track and attempt to delay Phileas Fogg's progress around the world, and whose Suez consul became Fix's confidant and strategic advisor in the pursuit.

memes.label: Calcutta
memes.description: Capital of British India and pivotal stop on Phileas Fogg's journey, where a courtroom drama at the hands of Judge Obadiah nearly ends the circumnavigation.

memes.label: Captain Andrew Speedy
memes.description: The irascible captain of the Henrietta, the steamer that Fogg commandeers for the Atlantic crossing -- a man whose name belies his reluctance to deviate from his scheduled course.

memes.label: Carnatic (Steamship)
memes.description: The P&O steamship Carnatic, which carries Passepartout — separated from Phileas Fogg in Hong Kong — alone and penniless to Yokohama, becoming a pivotal plot device in the novel's most intricate sequence of missed connections.

memes.label: Chicago
memes.description: The major American railway hub through which Phileas Fogg passes on his transcontinental journey from San Francisco to New York, connecting the Chicago and Rock Island Railroad to the eastern rail network.

memes.label: Circumnavigation
memes.description: The act of traveling all the way around the Earth — the central organizing premise of Phileas Fogg's eighty-day journey and the novel's framework for exploring the interconnected world of 1872.

memes.label: Colonel Stamp W. Proctor
memes.description: The belligerent American colonel encountered on the transcontinental train — a man whose violent temper triggers a confrontation with Fogg that is interrupted by a Sioux attack.

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Complete index of all key concepts and themes explored in Around the World in Eighty Days, from circumnavigation to timekeeping.

memes.label: Courage
memes.description: The quiet courage that Fogg displays — not the bravado of a swashbuckler but the steady nerve of a man who refuses to be intimidated by circumstance.

memes.label: The Crossing of America
memes.description: The segment of Phileas Fogg's journey across the North American continent — from San Francisco to New York — by the transcontinental railroad, disrupted by a Sioux attack, a near-duel, a buffalo stampede, and the obstacles of the western frontier.

memes.label: The Crossing of India
memes.description: The segment of Phileas Fogg's journey across the Indian subcontinent — from Bombay to Calcutta — which includes an unfinished railway, a ride on an elephant, and the rescue of Aouda from suttee.

memes.label: The Crossing of the Pacific
memes.description: The segment of Phileas Fogg's journey across the Pacific Ocean — from Yokohama to San Francisco — aboard the steamer General Grant, which passes without major incident but represents the longest single maritime leg of the circumnavigation.

memes.label: The Daily Telegraph
memes.description: The London newspaper that alone among the British press supported Phileas Fogg's eighty-day circumnavigation, publishing his itinerary and defending his project against widespread skepticism — and whose readership became the first constituency of believers that helped shift public opinion in Fogg's favor.

memes.label: Detective Fix
memes.description: Scotland Yard detective who pursues Phileas Fogg around the world, believing him to be the Bank of England robber.

memes.label: Detective Pursuit
memes.description: The parallel journey of Detective Fix, who pursues Phileas Fogg around the world believing him to be a bank robber — a narrative device that adds suspense, irony, and a second, hidden itinerary to the novel.

memes.label: Duty
memes.description: The moral obligation that compels Fogg to act — rescuing Aouda, pursuing Passepartout's captors, and honoring the wager regardless of consequence.

memes.label: Egypt
memes.description: The gateway between the Mediterranean and the Red Sea — the Isthmus of Suez, where Fogg's steamer the Mongolia stopped en route to Bombay and where Detective Fix first identified his quarry.

memes.label: Elder William Hitch
memes.description: The Mormon elder encountered on the transcontinental train through Utah — a proselytizer whose earnest discourse provides a colorful interlude on the American leg of the journey.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Complete index of all key events and incidents in Around the World in Eighty Days, from the Reform Club wager to the final arrival.

memes.label: Featured Pages
memes.description: The highest-quality pages in the wiki — featured and premium content that serves as the flagship showcase of Around the World in Eighty Days.

memes.label: Detective Fix
memes.description: The Scotland Yard detective dispatched to pursue the Bank of England robber — who, mistaking Phileas Fogg for the thief, follows him around the world in a relentless pursuit complicated by his inability to make an arrest on foreign soil.

memes.label: Fort Kearney
memes.description: A U.S. Army fort and railroad station in Nebraska, the site of a critical confrontation with attacking Sioux warriors during Fogg's transcontinental railway journey.

memes.label: Ganges River
memes.description: The sacred river of India, through whose valley Phileas Fogg travels by rail from Allahabad to Benares — a landscape of temples, pilgrims, and Hindu devotion that Fogg observes with characteristic mathematical indifference.

memes.label: Gauthier Ralph
memes.description: A Director of the Bank of England and member of the Reform Club, one of Phileas Fogg's regular whist partners and a participant in the famous wager discussion.

memes.label: Gentlemanly Conduct
memes.description: The Victorian code of masculine honour, emotional restraint, and financial integrity that defines Phileas Fogg's character and drives every major decision in the novel.

memes.label: Global Communication
memes.description: The global telegraph network — spanning continents and oceans — that enabled the rapid transmission of news, warrants, and rumors, shaping the pursuit of Phileas Fogg and the public perception of his journey.

memes.label: Great Indian Peninsula Railway
memes.description: The railway company whose partially completed line across India forced Phileas Fogg to improvise an overland elephant journey — the first significant deviation from his meticulously planned eighty-day itinerary. Sir Francis Cromarty, an experienced Anglo-Indian brigadier-general who knew the country intimately, was travelling on the same train and was himself astonished that the line was incomplete.

memes.label: Hong Kong
memes.description: The British colony where Fogg misses the Carnatic steamer and where Fix drugs Passepartout in an opium den — a pivotal transit point where the detective's pursuit reaches its most aggressive phase.

memes.label: The Illustrated London News
memes.description: The illustrated weekly newspaper whose publication of Phileas Fogg's portrait transformed him from an anonymous Reform Club member into a public figure, and which chronicled his journey from skepticism to vindication — reappearing at the novel's close as Fogg absorbed the news of his own triumph.

memes.label: Imperial Imagination
memes.description: The Victorian-era worldview that made an around-the-world journey conceivable as a gentleman's wager — reflecting British imperial infrastructure, colonial confidence, and the geographical assumptions of the 1870s, which the novel both embodies and subtly complicates.

memes.label: Imperial Infrastructure
memes.description: The network of railways, steamships, and telegraph lines that Britain built across its empire — the technological foundation that made Phileas Fogg's eighty-day circumnavigation possible.

memes.label: India Travel
memes.description: Pages related to travel through India in Around the World in Eighty Days, including locations, events, and characters encountered during Phileas Fogg's journey across the Indian subcontinent.

memes.label: Cultural Encounters in Around the World
memes.description: An analysis of how Phileas Fogg and his companions encounter — or fail to encounter — the cultures through which they pass, and what the novel's treatment of cultural difference reveals about the Victorian travel imagination.

memes.label: The Fictional Geography of Eighty Days
memes.description: How Verne's fictional route maps onto — and departs from — the actual transportation infrastructure of 1872, and what those departures reveal about the novel's narrative priorities.

memes.label: Jules Verne's Worldview in Eighty Days
memes.description: A critical analysis of the values, assumptions, and contradictions in Jules Verne's portrayal of technology, empire, and cultural difference in Around the World in Eighty Days.

memes.label: International Date Line
memes.description: The imaginary line at approximately 180° longitude where the calendar date changes — the geographical fact that saves Phileas Fogg from losing his wager by granting him an extra day when traveling eastward.

memes.label: James Forster
memes.description: Phileas Fogg's previous servant, dismissed on the morning of the journey for bringing shaving water at eighty-four degrees Fahrenheit instead of eighty-six — a cautionary figure whose fate drives Passepartout's anxiety.

memes.label: Jean Passepartout
memes.description: Jean Passepartout, the newly engaged French valet to Phileas Fogg, whose adaptability, curiosity, and occasional blunders both complicate and ultimately aid the journey around the world.

memes.label: John Bunsby
memes.description: Master of the pilot-boat Tankadere, who sailed Phileas Fogg from Hong Kong to Shanghai through treacherous waters in a daring voyage.

memes.label: John Sullivan
memes.description: A banker and member of the Reform Club, one of the five gentlemen who engage in the famous whist game during which Phileas Fogg's eighty-day circumnavigation wager is made.

memes.label: Judge Obadiah
memes.description: A judge in Bombay who presides over the court where Passepartout is tried for the Malabar Hill temple incident.

memes.label: Liverpool
memes.description: The port where Fogg is arrested by Fix -- the one place on British soil where the warrant is valid, and the site of the novel's greatest crisis.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The starting and finishing point of Fogg's journey — the imperial capital whose institutions, clubs, and infrastructure make the eighty-day wager plausible, and whose precise geography frames the novel's obsession with time.

memes.label: Lord Albemarle
memes.description: An elderly paralytic gentleman and Reform Club member who bets five thousand pounds on Fogg's success.

memes.label: Mongolia (Steamship)
memes.description: The P&O steamship Mongolia, which carries Phileas Fogg and Passepartout from Brindisi through the Suez Canal to Bombay — the first major leg of the eighty-day circumnavigation.

memes.label: Mudge
memes.description: An American guide hired by Phileas Fogg to pilot a sledge across the snowbound Nebraska plains, helping the party reach Omaha in time to catch the Chicago train.

memes.label: New York
memes.description: The American metropolis where Fogg misses the Liverpool steamer China, purchases the Henrietta from Captain Speedy, and makes his last improvisation before the Atlantic crossing — the final American waypoint in his eighty-day circuit.

memes.label: Ogden
memes.description: The railroad junction city in Utah Territory where Fogg's transcontinental train rested for six hours — a crucial node on the Pacific Railroad and the connecting point for the branch line to Salt Lake City.

memes.label: Omaha
memes.description: The Nebraska city that serves as the eastern terminus of the Union Pacific Railroad, connecting the transcontinental railway to the eastern United States network; a key transit point in Phileas Fogg's journey across America.

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Complete index of all organizations and institutions featured in Around the World in Eighty Days, from the Reform Club to railway companies.

memes.label: Peninsular and Oriental Steam Navigation Company
memes.description: The Peninsular and Oriental Steam Navigation Company — the shipping giant whose scheduled steamers carry Fogg across the Mediterranean, Indian Ocean, and South China Sea, making the eighty-day circumnavigation logistically possible.

memes.label: Pacific Mail Steamship Company
memes.description: The American shipping line whose General Grant carries Fogg across the Pacific from Yokohama to San Francisco.

memes.label: Passepartout
memes.description: Phileas Fogg's newly-hired French valet — an ex-acrobat, fireman, and jack-of-all-trades whose warmth, curiosity, and improvisational daring provide the novel's human heart and comic spirit.

memes.label: Passepartout in Yokohama
memes.description: Separated from Fogg and penniless in Japan, Passepartout joins a circus as an acrobat to survive until his master's arrival.

memes.label: Passport and Identity
memes.description: The passport as an instrument of identity and control in a world of borders — how Fogg's papers both enable his journey and make him vulnerable to Fix's pursuit.

memes.label: Character Index
memes.description: Complete index of all characters in Around the World in Eighty Days, sortable by name and quality tier.

memes.label: Phileas Fogg
memes.description: The enigmatic English gentleman who wagers half his fortune that he can travel around the world in eighty days — a man of mathematical precision, unshakeable calm, and hidden depths of courage and compassion.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Complete index of all places and locations featured in Around the World in Eighty Days, from London to the Pacific islands.

memes.label: Ports and Maritime Routes
memes.description: Port cities and maritime vessels encountered during the journey around the world, from Suez to the Atlantic crossing, including steamships and key harbours.

memes.label: Precision and Timekeeping
memes.description: Phileas Fogg's methodical relationship with time as the organising principle of the novel's plot and the key to its famous reversal at the International Date Line.

memes.label: Queenstown
memes.description: The Irish port at which transatlantic steamers historically stopped to drop off the mail before continuing to Liverpool — the critical waypoint where Fogg's Henrietta made its final landfall, gaining twelve precious hours via the Irish mail express.

memes.label: Railway Network
memes.description: Pages related to railways and train travel in Around the World in Eighty Days. Railways were the primary mode of overland transport for Fogg's journey.

memes.label: Railway Travel
memes.description: Steam railway travel as the land-based complement to ocean steamers in Fogg's circumnavigation, from the Great Indian Peninsula Railway through the American transcontinental line.

memes.label: Rangoon (Steamship)
memes.description: The P&O steamship Rangoon, which carries Fogg, Aouda, Passepartout, and Fix from Calcutta to Hong Kong via Singapore — the second major sea leg of the circumnavigation.

memes.label: The Reform Club
memes.description: The Pall Mall gentlemen's club where Phileas Fogg plays whist, stakes his fortune on an eighty-day circumnavigation, and must return to claim victory — the alpha and omega of the novel's geography and its social universe.

memes.label: The Reform Club Circle
memes.description: Pages related to the Reform Club and its members — the gentlemen's club in London where Phileas Fogg made his famous wager.

memes.label: Reverend Samuel Wilson
memes.description: The Protestant missionary in Hong Kong whose opium-den rescue of Passepartout provides one of the novel's most vivid set-pieces — a man of faith in a den of vice.

memes.label: Sacramento
memes.description: The capital of California and the starting point of the Central Pacific Railroad, which carried Phileas Fogg across the American continent from Sacramento eastward through the Sierra Nevada toward Omaha.

memes.label: Salt Lake City
memes.description: Salt Lake City, the Mormon capital on the shores of the Great Salt Lake, through which Fogg's transcontinental railway journey passes, offering a brief window into the Mormon settlement in the American West.

memes.label: Samuel Fallentin
memes.description: A banker and Reform Club member, one of the five whist partners who participate in Fogg's circumnavigation wager.

memes.label: San Francisco
memes.description: The booming Pacific port where Fogg lands after crossing from Yokohama — a former Gold Rush town transformed into a great commercial city, and the scene of the political rally where Fogg meets Colonel Stamp Proctor, setting up the novel's most violent confrontation.

memes.label: Saville Row
memes.description: Phileas Fogg's residence at No. 7, Saville Row, Burlington Gardens, London — the starting and ending point of his eighty-day journey around the world.

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The Metropolitan Police headquarters that dispatches Detective Fix on the global pursuit of a bank robber — a mission that Fix executes with more persistence than accuracy.

memes.label: Shanghai
memes.description: Shanghai, a major Chinese treaty port and the intended rendezvous point where Fogg's party hopes to catch the trans-Pacific steamer to Yokohama, but where timing proves critical.

memes.label: Singapore
memes.description: The island port of Singapore — a brief coaling stop on the Rangoon's voyage from Calcutta to Hong Kong, where Fogg and his party encounter the diversity of the British Empire in microcosm.

memes.label: Sir Francis Cromarty
memes.description: The British brigadier-general stationed in India who shares the journey from Bombay to Calcutta and becomes Fogg's enthusiastic ally in the rescue of Aouda from the suttee pyre.

memes.label: Spirit of Adventure
memes.description: The Victorian spirit of exploration and adventure that frames the novel's journey — Passepartout's motivation contrasted with Fogg's mechanical precision.

memes.label: Steam Technology
memes.description: The transformative steam power technology — locomotives and marine engines — that made high-speed global travel possible in the late nineteenth century, providing the mechanical foundation for Phileas Fogg's eighty-day journey.

memes.label: Steamer Travel
memes.description: Steam-powered ocean travel as the maritime backbone of Fogg's circumnavigation and a symbol of Victorian global connectivity.

memes.label: Steamships and Vessels
memes.description: The steamships and vessels that carry Phileas Fogg across the oceans and seas — from the Mongolia to the Henrietta — each playing a crucial role in the race against time.

memes.label: Suez
memes.description: The Egyptian port at the southern end of the Suez Canal where Fix first identifies Fogg and begins his pursuit.

memes.label: Suspicion and Trust
memes.description: The dynamic of suspicion and trust that drives the novel's detective subplot: Fix's mistaken suspicion of Fogg, Passepartout's misplaced trust in Fix, and Fogg's stoic indifference to the forces pursuing him.

memes.label: Tankadere
memes.description: The pilot-boat commanded by John Bunsby that carried Phileas Fogg, Aouda, and Passepartout from Hong Kong toward Shanghai through a typhoon — a desperate act of improvisation after they missed the Carnatic steamer.

memes.label: Technology and Innovation
memes.description: Steam power, telegraphy, railways, and precision timekeeping — the 19th-century innovations that enable Phileas Fogg's race against time in Around the World in Eighty Days.

memes.label: The Arrest at Liverpool
memes.description: Fix arrests Fogg on British soil — only to learn that the real bank robber has been caught three days earlier.

memes.label: The Bank of England Robbery
memes.description: The theft of £55,000 in banknotes from the Bank of England on 29 September 1872 — the crime that sends Detective Fix in pursuit of Phileas Fogg, initiating the cat-and-mouse chase that shadows the entire circumnavigation.

memes.label: The Elephant Journey
memes.description: When the railway proves incomplete, Fogg purchases an elephant and traverses the Indian jungle — the journey's most vivid detour.

memes.label: Henrietta (Steamship)
memes.description: The iron-hulled, wood-built steamship that Phileas Fogg commandeers for the Atlantic crossing from New York to Queenstown, after purchasing it outright from Captain Speedy when the captain refuses to divert to Liverpool.

memes.label: The Burning of the Henrietta
memes.description: When coal runs short, Fogg buys the steamer Henrietta and burns its wooden superstructure to fuel the Atlantic crossing.

memes.label: The Journey
memes.description: A comprehensive view of the circumnavigation route, the key locations, transport modes, and the concept of travelling around the world in eighty days.

memes.label: The Pacific Crossing
memes.description: The longest ocean leg of Fogg's journey — the Pacific crossing from Yokohama to San Francisco aboard the General Grant, spanning the world's largest ocean.

memes.label: The Parsee Guide
memes.description: The unnamed Parsee man who guides Fogg's party through the Indian jungle on elephant-back — a figure whose local knowledge enables the rescue of Aouda and the novel's most vivid passage through non-European space.

memes.label: The Rescue of Passepartout from the Sioux
memes.description: Fogg leads an armed party to rescue Passepartout from the Sioux, risking his life and his wager.

memes.label: The Rescue from Suttee
memes.description: In the jungles of Bundelkhand, Phileas Fogg diverts from his precious timetable to rescue Aouda, a young Parsi widow, from ritual immolation — the moment that transforms his journey from a race against time into a story of human connection.

memes.label: The Rescue of Aouda
memes.description: The rescue of Aouda from suttee in India — the pivotal moral act that transforms Fogg's journey from a mechanical race into a human endeavor.

memes.label: The Route
memes.description: The specific eighty-day itinerary that Phileas Fogg follows in his circumnavigation — a chain of railways and steamers linking London to the world and back again, published as a speculative route in the Daily Telegraph.

memes.label: The Sioux Attack
memes.description: A Sioux war party attacks the transcontinental train; Fogg organizes a defense, but Passepartout is captured.

memes.label: The Suez Canal
memes.description: The recently-completed Suez Canal -- the engineering marvel that makes Fogg's route through the Mediterranean to India possible, shortening the journey by thousands of miles.

memes.label: The Telegraph
memes.description: The global electric telegraph network that enables Detective Fix to pursue Fogg across continents — information traveling faster than any human conveyance.

memes.label: The Times
memes.description: The leading London newspaper that Phileas Fogg read daily at the Reform Club — a fixture of his methodical morning routine and a symbol of the established press that initially dismissed his eighty-day wager as madness.

memes.label: The Timetable
memes.description: The meticulously calculated eighty-day itinerary — published in the Daily Telegraph — that governed every move of Phileas Fogg's journey, transforming time itself into the novel's central antagonist.

memes.label: The Wager
memes.description: The £20,000 wager at the Reform Club that drives Phileas Fogg to attempt circumnavigating the globe in eighty days — the novel's central narrative engine and a lens through which honor, precision, and human connection are tested.

memes.label: The Wager at the Reform Club
memes.description: The inciting incident that launches the entire journey: at the Reform Club, Phileas Fogg wagers twenty thousand pounds — half his fortune — that he can circumnavigate the globe in eighty days, against the skepticism of five fellow whist players.

memes.label: Thomas Flanagan
memes.description: A brewer and member of the Reform Club, one of Phileas Fogg's regular whist partners and a participant in the famous eighty-day wager discussion.

memes.label: Time Pressure
memes.description: The constant urgency that drives Phileas Fogg's journey — every delay, every missed connection, and every unexpected obstacle threatens the eighty-day deadline and the £20,000 wager.

memes.label: Time Zones
memes.description: The division of the Earth into longitudinal zones with uniform clock times — the concept that provides the novel's dramatic climax when Fogg discovers he has "gained" a day by traveling eastward.

memes.label: Timetable
memes.description: The published schedules of railways, steamships, and coaches that made long-distance travel predictable in the nineteenth century — and upon which Phileas Fogg's eighty-day circumnavigation entirely depended.

memes.label: Transcontinental Railway
memes.description: The railroad line connecting San Francisco to New York — completed in 1869, just three years before Fogg's journey — that made the American leg of his circumnavigation possible.

memes.label: Transportation Methods
memes.description: Pages covering the various modes of transportation used during Phileas Fogg's circumnavigation — steamships, railways, sailing vessels, and more.

memes.label: Transportation Modes
memes.description: The variety of conveyances — steamers, railways, sailing vessels, elephants — that enabled Phileas Fogg's circumnavigation, showcasing the rapid transit technologies of the late nineteenth century.

memes.label: Yokohama
memes.description: The Japanese port where Passepartout, stranded and penniless after missing the Carnatic, joins a troupe of Long Nose acrobats to survive — and where Fogg, having crossed from Hong Kong on a pilot-boat, finds his servant in the midst of a collapsing human pyramid.
